_meta.hard_boundary: No personal microdata. Every figure derives from published open aggregates/cross-tabs (Census 2021 via Nomis; DfT VEH0135). Raw responses cached OUTSIDE the repo (~/.cache/se_coverage).
_meta.geography: England & Wales (Census 2021). Region = 9 English regions + Wales.

provenance_legend.observed: jointly measured in a real open source
provenance_legend.observed-marginal: real source but marginal/area-level only, no household joint

_meta.measure: Cramers V (categorical x categorical), aggregate over whole E&W population.
_meta.CRITICAL_interpretation: aggregate_band is the WHOLE-POPULATION association. tail_max_lift = max_cell P(col|row)/P(col) (col marginal>=0.5%). A pair can read 'orthogonal' in aggregate yet be strongly coupled in the TAILS (e.g. heatingfuel_x_region V=0.070 'orthogonal' but oil-only ranges 0.12% London to 7.83% Wales = ~65x). Because the coverage design's objective is the WORST CELL, the aggregate V is the WRONG lens for deciding what to cross vs collapse -- use tail lift. This is the load-bearing stage-2 finding.

tail_max_lift.cell: Owned: Owns outright | A caravan or other mobile or temporary structure

tail_min_lift.cell: Owned: Owns with a mortgage or loan or shared ownership | A caravan or other mobile or temporary structure

tail_max_lift.cell: Does not have central heating | A caravan or other mobile or temporary structure

tail_min_lift.cell: Does not have central heating | Whole house or bungalow: Detached

tail_max_lift.cell: Rented: Private rented or lives rent free | Does not have central heating

tail_min_lift.cell: Owned: Owns with a mortgage or loan or shared ownership | Does not have central heating

tail_max_lift.cell: L15: Full-time students | Rented: Private rented or lives rent free

tail_min_lift.cell: L15: Full-time students | Owned: Owns outright

tail_max_lift.cell: No cars or vans in household | Rented: Social rented

tail_min_lift.cell: No cars or vans in household | Owned: Owns with a mortgage or loan or shared ownership

tail_max_lift.cell: London | District or communal heat networks only
